observations.summary: The motif of stars as a source of original light and aspiration is echoed in Dan's dreams of climbing and rising towards the sky, symbolizing a spiritual ascent and longing for transcendence.

source_evidence.quote: Through the wall slit, the stars of Hebron burned in their old patterns above the hills. His mother had taught him they were the closest thing to the unseen source—that original light from which everything borrowed its brief existence and to which everything returned. And his dreams had always found their way there. Not the shapeless kind. Deliberate. He had dreamed of climbing endless stairs in the dark, his legs never tiring, the air growing thinner and cleaner with every step. Of jumping and not coming back down. Of spreading his arms and rising until the whole dark earth curved below him like a broken plate.

observations.target_hint: Dan's spiritual journey and dreams in other chapters reflect this motif of ascent towards the stars and original light.
